summary: Attempted to decompile three grcastle functions, `grCastle_801CD960`, `grCastle_801CF750`, and `fn_801CFAFC`, with the PR body reporting clean `ninja` verification and 100.0 fuzzy match for all three. The PR was closed unmerged after the work was reportedly consolidated into other PRs. The main technical issue surfaced in comments was unsafe-looking pointer arithmetic caused by incomplete or mismatched Ground/GroundVars structure knowledge.
what_changed: Replaced the nonmatching placeholder for `grCastle_801CD960` with a loop over 12 entries starting at offset `0xE0` from `Ground*`, calling `grLib_801C9B6C((u8*) gp + 0xE0 + i * 0x14)`.

Replaced the nonmatching placeholder for `grCastle_801CF750` with logic selecting `idx` from `arg1`, checking `cd->x34_flags.b1234 == 1`, offsetting the `Ground*` by `idx * 16`, and adding `arg3` as an `f32` into offset `0xD0`.

Replaced the nonmatching placeholder for `fn_801CFAFC` with a typed item/ground/fighter callback that writes `4` into `gp->gv.castle2.xC4` via an `s16*` cast and, if `ftLib_80086960(gobj)` succeeds, calls `ftLib_80086A4C(gobj, (f32) grCs_804D6970->x4)`.
Updated `grcastle.h` to include `<melee/it/forward.h>` so `Item_GObj*` could be used in the typed prototype.
Changed the header prototype for `fn_801CFAFC` from `UNK_RET fn_801CFAFC(UNK_PARAMS)` to `void fn_801CFAFC(Item_GObj*, Ground*, Vec3*, HSD_GObj*, f32)`.
The PR was not merged; a later comment says the work was consolidated into other PRs.
smart_moves: Kept the three newly decompiled functions small and local, only adding header typing where needed for the newly known `fn_801CFAFC` signature.
Preserved match-sensitive pointer arithmetic where the known structs did not yet provide reliable named fields, rather than inventing inaccurate struct members.
Flagged that replacing the casts with field access was non-trivial because the existing castle `GroundVars` layout did not match the observed access pattern.
Used explicit forward include support for `Item_GObj` instead of relying on undeclared or generic callback parameters.
decomp_lessons: A 100% matching decomp may still be structurally unsatisfactory if it depends on raw offsets caused by incomplete shared struct layouts.

For stage code, especially `Ground`/`GroundVars`, do not force field names when the observed binary behavior treats a region differently from the current C type. In this PR, offset `0xE0` was typed as a 4-byte pointer in the existing structure, but the code treated it as an inline array of 12 elements of size `0x14`.

`grCastle_801CF750` showed a case where the code offsets the `Ground*` itself by `idx * 16` before writing to offset `0xD0`; this may imply a broader layout or aliasing issue rather than a simple missing field.
When pointer math points at shared type problems, a correct cleanup may require coordinated changes to shared `Ground`/`GroundVars` definitions instead of a local stage-only patch.
Closed/unmerged PRs can still capture useful matching tactics and layout caveats, but downstream users should verify whether the final version landed through the referenced consolidated PRs.
naming_conventions: Stage functions use the `grCastle_` prefix for castle stage routines.
Local callback-style functions in this file can retain the `fn_` prefix when no semantic name is known, as with `fn_801CFAFC` and `fn_801CFB68`.

Typed prototypes prefer concrete project typedefs such as `Item_GObj*`, `Ground*`, `Vec3*`, `HSD_GObj*`, `CollData*`, and `mpLib_GroundEnum` instead of `UNK_PARAMS` once call signatures are understood.
The global `grCs_804D6970` follows the stage-specific `grCs_` prefix seen in the diff.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used byte-pointer casts, e.g. `(u8*) gp + offset`, to express exact offset arithmetic needed for matching.
Used a `do while` loop in `grCastle_801CD960` to match iteration over 12 fixed-size entries at stride `0x14`.
Used `PAD_STACK(16)` in `grCastle_801CF750`, indicating stack-shape preservation was needed for the match.
Used explicit casts such as `(s32) cd->x34_flags.b1234 == 1`, `(Ground*) ((u8*) gp + idx * 16)`, `*(f32*) ((u8*) gp + 0xD0)`, and `*(s16*) &gp->gv.castle2.xC4` to preserve codegen.
Converted `arg3` to float before accumulation in `grCastle_801CF750`, matching the diff's `(f32) arg3` behavior.

Kept `fn_801CFAFC`'s unused parameters in the typed signature, likely reflecting the ABI/callback signature even though only `gp`, `gobj`, and `arg4`-adjacent context are meaningful in the shown body.

review_feedback.feedback: Author/commenter noted they would fix pointer math soon.

Pointer math in `grCastle_801CD960` and `grCastle_801CF750` could not be easily replaced with struct field access because the castle `GroundVars` struct lacked appropriate named fields at the accessed offsets.
Offset `0xE0` was described as currently typed as a 4-byte pointer while the code treated it as an inline array of 0x14-byte elements.
`grCastle_801CF750` was described as shifting the `Ground` pointer itself by `idx * 16`, suggesting cleanup would need structural changes to shared `Ground`/`GroundVars` types.
Final comment says the work was consolidated into other PRs, explaining the closed-unmerged state.

follow_up_queries: Find the later PRs that consolidated or superseded PR #2248's `grCastle_801CD960`, `grCastle_801CF750`, and `fn_801CFAFC` work.
Inspect current `GroundVars` and castle-specific ground variable structs around offsets `0xC4`, `0xD0`, and `0xE0` to see whether the raw pointer arithmetic was eventually replaced with named fields.
Search for other stage files using `grLib_801C9B6C` over inline arrays with stride `0x14` to infer a shared struct type.
Check whether any final merged version retained `PAD_STACK(16)` in `grCastle_801CF750` or found a cleaner expression that still matched.
